identification.title: Next.js middleware authorization bypass via x-middleware-subrequest

vulnerability.description: Next.js 14.x mishandles the x-middleware-subrequest header, allowing attackers to skip middleware auth checks and access protected API routes without valid credentials.
vulnerability.root_cause: Internal middleware subrequest handling trusts x-middleware-subrequest to denote nested middleware invocations; attacker-controlled values bypass the middleware chain.
vulnerability.attack_scenario: Send GET /api/hello without Authorization but with x-middleware-subrequest set to a nested middleware chain (e.g. middleware repeated 5 times separated by colons) to skip auth and read the protected response body.

primitives.name: protected_api_read
primitives.description: Protected /api/hello returns JSON message when bypass succeeds

endpoints.notes: Middleware requires Authorization: my-jwt-token-here. Without bypass, returns 401 Unauthorized JSON.

source_artifacts.patch_diff: Vendor fix: reject or sanitize x-middleware-subrequest from external clients in affected Next.js versions.

flag.location_detail: JSON field message in GET /api/hello when auth bypass succeeds

success_signals.description: Protected endpoint body leaked without valid Authorization

metadata.author: kavach
metadata.notes: Start lab: cd kavach/lab/vulnerable-nextjs-14-CVE-2025-29927 && npx next dev -p 3100. Run: python run.py --cve-json data/examples/lab_nextjs_cve_2025_29927.json --target http://127.0.0.1:3100/
